message:
perf(framebuffer): replace per-pixel division with reciprocal multiply in draw_column_shaded

Precompute 1/height reciprocal and light delta outside the per-pixel loop
in both doom and quake draw_column_shaded. Also hoist base color f32
conversions. Replaces per-pixel division with multiplication, saving one
expensive fdiv per pixel in the column shading inner loop.

Closes bd-1b13z.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-1awh","title":"Implement Widget Introspection Debug Overlay tests","description":"# Implement Widget Introspection Debug Overlay tests

## Background
Part of Widget Introspection Debug Overlay (bd-17nn). Tests ensure debug overlay works correctly.

## Unit Tests
- overlay_shows_widget_hierarchy: Tree view displays correctly
- selection_highlights_widget: Selected widget is visually marked
- properties_panel_shows_correct_data: Widget properties displayed accurately
- toggle_on_off_works: F12 (or configured key) toggles overlay
- overlay_does_not_affect_underlying_render: Original content unchanged

## Integration Tests
- hover_over_widget_shows_inspector: Mouse over activates inspector
- inspector_traverses_widget_tree: Can navigate up/down hierarchy
- overlay_works_with_complex_layouts: Nested layouts handled correctly

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All unit tests pass
- [ ] Integration tests verify real widget introspection
- [ ] Performance overhead < 1ms when overlay disabled","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-01T03:32:45.595414674Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-01T21:12:58.483153232Z","closed_at":"2026-02-01T21:12:58.483135769Z","close_reason":"Added debug overlay unit tests in crates/ftui-widgets/src/debug_overlay.rs","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1awh","depends_on_id":"bd-17nn","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T14:32:16Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"bd-1b5h","title":"Demo Showcase: Live Log Search + Filter","description":"## Background
The log viewer is a core demo. Live search/filtering makes it feel powerful and real‑world.

## Scope / Goals
- Typeahead filtering with match highlighting
- Regex vs literal modes
- Case sensitivity toggle
- Optional context lines around matches
- Smooth behavior in inline mode (no flicker)

## Deliverables
- Search UI + filter pipeline
- Highlight spans in LogViewer
- Integration in demo showcase
- Tests: unit, snapshot, perf, PTY E2E
- Docs + demo script

## Testing & Logging
- Unit tests for filter correctness and highlights
- Snapshot tests for key states
- Perf tests with large logs (10k+ lines)
- PTY E2E with verbose logging (query + result counts)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Search is fast and deterministic
- [ ] Highlighting accurate across modes
- [ ] Inline mode remains stable
- [ ] All tests pass

## Success Criteria
- Epic delivers a cohesive user-visible capability: Demo Showcase: Live Log Search + Filter
- Child tasks are not just implemented; each has unit tests + E2E scripts with verbose JSONL logs and deterministic fixtures
- Graceful degradation tiers are specified (capabilities, fallbacks, safety) and validated in tests


## Alien Artifact Upgrades
- Model the core decision(s) with explicit state, priors/assumptions, and invariants; write down failure modes as first-class artifacts.
- Explainability: add an evidence ledger for any non-trivial decision (why this branch, why this fallback). Prefer Bayes factors / log-likelihood contributions over ad-hoc scoring.
- Guarantees: where prediction/thresholding exists, prefer distribution-free calibration (conformal prediction) or anytime-valid testing (e-process) so we can stop early without invalid inference.
- Graceful degradation: define capability tiers + deterministic fallbacks; never fail closed without a traceable reason.


## Extreme Optimization
- Baseline first: capture p50/p95/p99 for relevant command(s) with `hyperfine` (store raw output).
- Profile next: CPU flamegraph + allocation profile; identify top-5 hotspots.
- Opportunity matrix: implement only Score ≥ 2.0 (Impact×Confidence/Effort).
- Isomorphism proof per change: ordering, tie-breaking, RNG seeds, and golden checksums must match.
- Regression gates: add microbench + budgets; failures must print actionable deltas and include log artifacts.

 {"id":"bd-1b5h.1","title":"Log Search spec + UX flow","description":"## UX / Behavior Spec
- Open search: `/` (consistent with vim-like search)
- Close: `Esc` (restores full log)
- Modes: Literal / Regex; Case Sensitive toggle
- Context lines: 0/1/2/5 lines around matches
- Empty query shows full log + hint
- No results state displays message + help

## Highlighting Rules
- All matches highlighted in list view
- Current match (if navigation added) is stronger highlight
- Highlights must not corrupt markup/ANSI

## Tests (Required)
- Unit: regex vs literal correctness
- Unit: case sensitivity toggle
- Snapshot: empty, matches, no results
- PTY E2E: open → type → toggle mode → exit

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Clear behavior in all modes
- [ ] Highlighting is correct and legible
- [ ] Keybindings documented in help overlay

## Alien Artifact Upgrades
- Model the core decision(s) with explicit state, priors/assumptions, and invariants; write down failure modes as first-class artifacts.
- Explainability: add an evidence ledger for any non-trivial decision (why this branch, why this fallback). Prefer Bayes factors / log-likelihood contributions over ad-hoc scoring.
- Guarantees: where prediction/thresholding exists, prefer distribution-free calibration (conformal prediction) or anytime-valid testing (e-process) so we can stop early without invalid inference.
- Graceful degradation: define capability tiers + deterministic fallbacks; never fail closed without a traceable reason.


## Extreme Optimization
- Baseline first: capture p50/p95/p99 for relevant command(s) with `hyperfine` (store raw output).
- Profile next: CPU flamegraph + allocation profile; identify top-5 hotspots.
- Opportunity matrix: implement only Score ≥ 2.0 (Impact×Confidence/Effort).
- Isomorphism proof per change: ordering, tie-breaking, RNG seeds, and golden checksums must match.
- Regression gates: add microbench + budgets; failures must print actionable deltas and include log artifacts.


## Testing Plan
- Unit tests: happy path + edge cases + regression fixtures.
- Property tests: invariants (idempotence, monotonicity, determinism) where applicable.
- Fuzz tests: parsers/state machines and any input-facing logic.
- Determinism: fixed seeds/time-freeze; snapshot fixtures must be stable.

 {"id":"bd-1yvn","title":"Integrate budget checking into frame loop","description":"# Integrate budget checking into frame loop

## Background
Part of Render Budget Enforcement (bd-1iqn). The frame loop must create budgets, check them during rendering, and handle exhaustion.

## Technical Design

```rust
impl<M: Model> Program<M> {
    pub fn run_frame(&mut self, terminal: &mut Terminal) -> io::Result<()> {
        // Create budget for this frame
        let mut budget = RenderBudget::new(self.config.budget.total);
        
        // Track consecutive overruns for degradation cooldown
        static OVERRUN_COUNT: AtomicU32 = AtomicU32::new(0);
        static STABLE_COUNT: AtomicU32 = AtomicU32::new(0);
        
        // Render phase with budget
        let frame = self.render_with_budget(&mut budget);
        
        // Check for degradation need
        if budget.should_degrade(self.config.budget.cost_estimates.unicode_border) {
            if OVERRUN_COUNT.fetch_add(1, Ordering::Relaxed) >= self.config.budget.degradation_cooldown {
                budget.degrade();
                OVERRUN_COUNT.store(0, Ordering::Relaxed);
                STABLE_COUNT.store(0, Ordering::Relaxed);
            }
        } else {
            STABLE_COUNT.fetch_add(1, Ordering::Relaxed);
        }
        
        // Check for upgrade opportunity
        if budget.should_upgrade() && STABLE_COUNT.load(Ordering::Relaxed) >= self.config.budget.upgrade_threshold {
            budget.upgrade();
            STABLE_COUNT.store(0, Ordering::Relaxed);
        }
        
        // Present phase
        if !budget.exhausted() {
            self.presenter.present_with_budget(frame, &budget)?;
        } else if self.config.budget.log_overruns {
            tracing::warn!(
                elapsed_ms = budget.elapsed().as_millis(),
                degradation = ?budget.degradation(),
                "Frame budget exhausted, skipping present"
            );
        }
        
        // Record metrics
        self.metrics.record_frame(budget.elapsed(), budget.degradation());
        
        Ok(())
    }
    
    fn render_with_budget(&mut self, budget: &mut RenderBudget) -> Frame {
        let mut frame = Frame::new(self.terminal_size);
        
        // Pass budget to view so widgets can check it
        let model_view = self.model.view(budget);
        
        // Render widget tree with budget awareness
        model_view.render(frame.area(), &mut frame.buffer, budget);
        
        frame
    }
}
```

## Key Design Decisions
1. **Cooldown prevents oscillation**: Dont degrade on single overrun
2. **Upgrade requires stability**: Must be under budget for N frames
3. **Frame skip is last resort**: Only when allow_frame_skip enabled
4. **Metrics recorded**: For observability and tuning

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Frame loop creates RenderBudget per frame
- [ ] Budget passed to render and present phases
- [ ] Cooldown logic prevents oscillation
- [ ] Upgrade logic requires stable performance
- [ ] Frame skip when exhausted
- [ ] Metrics recorded for every frame","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-01T03:16:21.317947151Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-01T20:56:38.986031743Z","closed_at":"2026-02-01T20:56:38.985962553Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1yvn","depends_on_id":"bd-389n","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T14:32:17Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1yvn","depends_on_id":"bd-3tfu","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T14:32:17Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-1yzfb","title":"Tests: PTY E2E Mermaid Rendering + JSONL Logs","description":"Background
- We need end-to-end validation under real terminal conditions.

Scope
- PTY E2E scripts rendering Mermaid diagrams at multiple sizes + glyph modes.
- Log JSONL: run_id, size, diagram_type, tier, glyph_mode, cache_hit, checksum, render_time_ms, warnings, guard_triggers, config_hash, init_config_hash, capability_profile, link_count, link_mode, legend_height.
- Include per-stage timings (parse_ms, layout_ms, route_ms, render_ms).
- Deterministic mode to compare outputs across runs.

Considerations
- Use scripted inputs to avoid nondeterminism.
- Ensure logs capture failures without crashing.

Success Criteria
- PTY run completes with stable checksums.
- JSONL logs include all required fields.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"OliveMeadow","created_at":"2026-02-04T18:40:47.353793891Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-07T05:39:05.181096786Z","closed_at":"2026-02-07T05:39:05.181071088Z","close_reason":"Completed:  {"id":"bd-1z02","title":"Optional OpenTelemetry Audit Trail","description":"Add optional OTEL plumbing (via tracing) so apps can export runtime spans/decisions to a collector without default performance cost.

## Success Criteria
- Epic delivers a cohesive user-visible capability: Optional OpenTelemetry Audit Trail
- Child tasks are not just implemented; each has unit tests + E2E scripts with verbose JSONL logs and deterministic fixtures
- Graceful degradation tiers are specified (capabilities, fallbacks, safety) and validated in tests
- Epic includes a perf budget + regression suite (hyperfine + flamegraphs + CI gates) with explainable failures


## Alien Artifact Upgrades
- Model the core decision(s) with explicit state, priors/assumptions, and invariants; write down failure modes as first-class artifacts.
- Explainability: add an evidence ledger for any non-trivial decision (why this branch, why this fallback). Prefer Bayes factors / log-likelihood contributions over ad-hoc scoring.
- Guarantees: where prediction/thresholding exists, prefer distribution-free calibration (conformal prediction) or anytime-valid testing (e-process) so we can stop early without invalid inference.
- Graceful degradation: define capability tiers + deterministic fallbacks; never fail closed without a traceable reason.
- Drift/Regimes: if runtime metrics vary (resize storms, frame pacing), incorporate change-point detection and regime-specific policies.


## Extreme Optimization
- Baseline first: capture p50/p95/p99 for relevant command(s) with `hyperfine` (store raw output).
- Profile next: CPU flamegraph + allocation profile; identify top-5 hotspots.
- Opportunity matrix: implement only Score ≥ 2.0 (Impact×Confidence/Effort).
- Isomorphism proof per change: ordering, tie-breaking, RNG seeds, and golden checksums must match.
- Regression gates: add microbench + budgets; failures must print actionable deltas and include log artifacts.
- Add JSONL perf logs with schema: run_id, case, env, features, seed, metrics, budgets, pass/fail, and artifact paths.


## Testing Plan
- Unit tests: happy path + edge cases + regression fixtures.
- Property tests: invariants (idempotence, monotonicity, determinism) where applicable.
- Fuzz tests: parsers/state machines and any input-facing logic.
- Determinism: fixed seeds/time-freeze; snapshot fixtures must be stable.

Full opt-in telemetry with zero overhead when disabled.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["design","performance","telemetry","tracing"],"comments":[{"id":346,"issue_id":"bd-1z02","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Background
- User feedback asks for an optional, high‑level audit trail of agent decisions and runtime spans, exported to an OTEL collector.
- Requirement: performance‑oriented tracing is not the goal; the desired value is observability and auditability.

Goals
- Provide opt‑in OTEL export via env vars (e.g., OTEL_HTTP_ENDPOINT, OTEL_TRACE_ID, OTEL_PARENT_SPAN_ID).
- Ensure zero overhead by default: feature‑gated dependencies, and no initialization unless env is set.
- Allow consumers to attach FrankenTUI spans to an existing trace if IDs are provided.

Non‑Goals
- Do not force a global tracing subscriber on library users.
- Do not introduce blocking network calls in the hot path.

Risks / Considerations
- Tracing subscriber interactions can conflict with app‑level tracing if not carefully scoped.
- OTEL exporters can be heavy; must be optional and well‑documented.

Deliverables
- Architecture and API spec for telemetry initialization.
- Implementation with feature flags and tests.
- Docs explaining env vars and how to integrate into host apps.
","created_at":"2026-02-02T22:14:20Z"},{"id":347,"issue_id":"bd-1z02","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Plan Update
